His research has specialized in electrochemistry and techno-economic modeling of economically efficient ways to reduce carbon dioxide emissions. Finke also helped commercialize an electrochemical wastewater treatment technology for applications in low-income countries.

companies.name: Cypris Materials
companies.one_liner: Cypris Materials is developing a new generation of sustainable color that broadens the color gamut while simplifying manufacturing, application and formulation.
companies.elevator_pitch: Cypris Materials is an advanced materials company founded to re-invent humans' relationship with color. Its vision is to bring nature's structural color to the market as a sustainable solution to global energy inefficiencies and alternative to toxic pigments and dyes. This short video shows the formulation and simple application of Cypris' invisible inks which magically form color: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bFQWq-pbwpE

nsf_awards.title: SBIR Phase II:  Structural Color for Sustainable Printing
SBIR Phase I:  Paintable Solar Reflective Coatings for Cool Roof Retrofits

fellow_profiles.name: Matthew Ryan
fellow_profiles.bio: Cypris Materials co-founder Matthew Ryan earned a Ph.D. in materials chemistry at Colorado State University. At Cypris, he leads the technical development of paintable nanostructured materials. His expertise encompasses catalysis, organic chemistry, and polymer-based photonic crystal materials. He holds a B.S. in biochemistry and molecular biology from UC Santa Cruz.

fellow_profiles.name: Ryan Pearson
fellow_profiles.bio: Cypris Materials co-founder Ryan Pearson earned a Ph.D. in materials chemistry at Colorado State University, where his thesis work focused on synthetic polymer chemistry, catalysis, and computational-directed discovery. Pearson's drive to lead a start-up is rooted in his efforts to promote green chemistry and bring sustainable technologies from academic ideas to market. Pearson earned a B.S. in chemistry from Penn State University.

companies.name: InchFab
companies.one_liner: Inchfab is a foundry for beyond-compute chip fabrication that offers ultra-fast cycle time.
companies.elevator_pitch: Inchfab provides rapid AIoT chip manufacturing services. Using a proprietary, AI/ML-driven microfabrication platform, Inchfab reduces customer cycle time by 10x, reducing time to market in addition to development and production costs. To meet the demands of today's environment where computing is moving toward specialized applications which require custom chips, Inchfab's $1M tabletop platform disrupts the existing $BB ecosystem and provides a scalable and economical fabrication solution.

nsf_awards.title: SBIR Phase I:  An Agile Microfabrication Platform for High-Mix, Low-Volume Microchip Production

fellow_profiles.name: Mitchell Hsing
fellow_profiles.bio: Mitchell Hsing co-founded Inchfab in 2019 to commercialize low-cost fabrication tools and processes for "more-than-Moore" micro- and nanoscale devices and provide a new, flexible alternative to existing manufacturing options. He earned a Ph.D. from Microsystems Technology Laboratory at MIT and his undergraduate degrees in electrical engineering and physics from UC Irvine.

fellow_profiles.name: Parker Gould
fellow_profiles.bio: Parker Gould co-founded InchFab in 2019 to commercialize low-cost fabrication tools and processes for "more-than-Moore" micro- and nanoscale devices and provide a new, flexible alternative to existing manufacturing options. He earned his Ph.D. from MIT's Microsystems Technology Laboratory, a M.Phil. in micro- and nanotechnology enterprise from the University of Cambridge, and a B.S. in electrical engineering from Vanderbilt University.

companies.name: Perceptra Technologies
companies.one_liner: Preceptra develops optical analyzers for realtime chemical monitoring in pharmaceutical and medical applications.
companies.elevator_pitch: Preceptra is developing optical analyzers for realtime chemical monitoring. It is applying its sensor platform to a host of applications ranging from biomanufacturing and precision agriculture to health monitoring.

nsf_awards.title: SBIR Phase I: High-Performance Integrated Photonic Raman Analyzers

fellow_profiles.name: Amir Atabaki
fellow_profiles.bio: Prior to founding Perceptra Technologies, Amir Atabaki was a research scientist at Research Laboratory of Electronics, MIT, where he worked on the integration of photonics and CMOS electronics for next-generation system-on-a-chip optical sensing and interconnect solutions. He received his Ph.D. from Georgia Tech, and his M.S. and B.S. in electrical engineering from Sharif University of Technology, Iran.

companies.name: Reveel
companies.one_liner: Reveel is disrupting fast fashion. With patent-pending MagicInk™️ personalized apparel is accessible, scalable, and environmentally conscious.
companies.elevator_pitch: Reveel is re-imagining fashion for the digital era. Download, print, and share your designs or NFTs in the real world with Reveel graphic t-shirt printer with color-changing MagicInk, then erase the design and print a new one. Infinite possibilities on one canvas.

fellow_profiles.name: Raj Bhakta
fellow_profiles.bio: Raj Bhakta studied smart textiles in Jesse Jur's research group at the North Carolina State University's Wilson College of Textiles, where he earned a doctorate in fiber and polymer science. Bhakta earned a B.S. in physics from the University of Texas-Austin and sees textiles as a huge white-space opportunity for innovation.

companies.name: SkyGig
companies.one_liner: SkyGig is developing advanced mmWave technology for 5G and satellite wireless communications markets.
companies.elevator_pitch: With the rising demands of data intensive applications, there is a need for more advanced wireless technologies to enable the emerging 5G/6G telecommunication infrastructures as well as satellite communications system. Through innovative holistic approaches to the wireless radio technology, SkyGig is bringing a whole new class of wireless systems and architecture to the market that provide significant cost-savings and performance boost for the operators and service providers.

nsf_awards.title: SBIR Phase II: Power-Optimized Beamforming Front-End Technology for High-Capacity Millimeter-Wave Wireless Communications
SBIR Phase II:  High Performance Millimeter-Wave Radiofrequency (RF) Front-End Technology for Multi-Gigabit Wireless Communications
SBIR Phase I:  Power-Optimized Beamforming Front-End Technology for High-Capacity Millimeter-Wave Wireless Communications
SBIR Phase I:  High Performance Millimeter-Wave RF Front-End Technology for Multi-Gigabit Wireless Communications

fellow_profiles.name: Armin Jam
fellow_profiles.bio: Before co-founding SkyGig with Avish Kosari, Armin Jam was a research fellow at the University of Michigan, where he earned his Ph.D. and M.Sc. in electrical engineering. He specializes in millimeter-wave antennas and fabrication; RF integration and packaging; as well as high-frequency instrumentation. He has demonstrated the most power-efficient beamforming RF front-end reported at sub-millimeter-wave frequencies and has been awarded in several international venues.

fellow_profiles.name: Avish Kosari
fellow_profiles.bio: Before co-founding SkyGig with Armin Jam, Avish Kosari earned her Ph.D. and M.Sc. in electrical engineering from the University of Michigan, where she performed postdoctoral research on advanced millimeter-wave wireless technologies. She specializes in low-power mixed-signal and RF integrated circuits as well as high-performance wireless radio systems and architectures. Her research focuses on the gap between power consumption and performance in wireless systems and has resulted in new and record-performance transceiver wireless architectures.

companies.name: Synopic
companies.tagline: A new perspective on smart imaging
companies.one_liner: Synopic is synergistically merging image, machine learning, and visualization technologies to enhance how we understand and interact with the world.
companies.elevator_pitch: 3D cameras are being integrated into many facets of our personal and professional lives, from automotive to entertainment to industry. In healthcare, however, current 3D camera technologies are limited by trade-offs in size, range, and visualization capabilities, limiting their use in many procedures. While many of the 15 million laparoscopic procedures per year enjoy the benefits of 3D imaging, endoscopic surgeries are too space-constrained for current 3D technology. Synopic is developing a new type of compact 3D camera with a single viewpoint, capable of scaling with endoscopes for real-time, high-resolution color imaging for better screening & diagnosis and improved procedure outcomes.

nsf_awards.title: SBIR Phase II: AI-driven 3D imaging and visualization for endoscopy
STTR Phase I:  FlatCam: Inexpensive, Compact Lensless Cameras for IoT Applications

fellow_profiles.name: Jesse Adams
fellow_profiles.bio: Jesse Adams earned his Ph.D. in applied physics from Rice University where he designed and tested novel cameras using computational imaging for both photography and microscopy with applications ranging from three-dimensional imaging to recording neural activity to face detection. While at Rice University, he worked with Professor Ashok Veeraraghavan with whom he co-founded Synopic to continue developing ground-breaking, computationally driven imaging technology.

companies.name: terrafuse
companies.tagline: Actionable climate intelligence for a resilient earth
companies.one_liner: Terrafuse is building data fusion technology to re-engineer the climate and weather risk prediction infrastructure around next-generation AI models.

nsf_awards.title: SBIR Phase II:  Physics-Informed Machine Learning Emulators to Model Physical Spatio-Temporal Processes for Climate and Weather Risk Forecasting
SBIR Phase I:  Machine learning emulators of weather and hydroclimate models for operational and financial risk assessment

fellow_profiles.name: Adrian Albert
fellow_profiles.bio: Adrian Albert, who died tragically on June 5, 2020, founded Terrafuse in 2018 to improve climate and weather risk prediction infrastructure. Before that, he was a machine learning research scientist at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory, designing machine learning algorithms incorporating physical knowledge for scientific applications. Albert earned his Ph.D. in electrical engineering at Stanford. At startup C3.ai, he worked on AI methods for energy and industrial systems.

companies.name: ActiveMEMS
companies.tagline: Powering the next generation of the IoT
companies.one_liner: ActiveMEMS is developing advanced microfabrication technology to integrate high-quality piezoelectric materials and transducers into MEMS manufacturing.

nsf_awards.title: SBIR Phase I:  Advanced Micro Vibration Energy Harvesters for Energy-Autonomous Internet of Things

fellow_profiles.name: Erkan Aktakka
universities: University of Michigan Distinguished Achievement Award
fellow_profiles.bio: Erkan Aktakka founded ActiveMEMS to commercialize his decade-long research on micro-electro-mechanical systems (MEMS) at the University of Michigan, Ann Arbor, where he received his Ph.D. Aktakka received the University of Michigan Distinguished Achievement Award and DTE Clean Energy Prize and has served as a Technical Program Committee member at the international conferences of PowerMEMS and IEEE Sensors.

companies.name: Antora Energy
companies.one_liner: Antora Energy is electrifying heavy industry with thermal energy storage for zero-carbon heat and power.

nsf_awards.title: SBIR Phase II: Research and development of production-scale high-efficiency Thermal Photovoltaic (TPV) cells to enable ultra-low cost energy storage.
STTR Phase I:  Advanced Thermophotovoltaic Generators for High-Value Remote Power

fellow_profiles.name: Andrew Ponec
fellow_profiles.bio: Antora Energy CEO Andrew Ponec received his B.S. in energy systems engineering from Stanford. Between his sophomore and junior year, Ponec took a leave to found a solar energy company called Dragonfly Systems. SunPower acquired Dragonfly in 2014. Ponec led his team through concept generation, prototyping, validation testing, and manufacturing, and eventually oversaw the installation of over 10 MW of solar power plants that used the technology.

fellow_profiles.name: Justin Briggs
fellow_profiles.bio: Justin Briggs holds a B.S. in physics from UC Berkeley and a Ph.D. in applied physics from Stanford University. During his Ph.D., Justin developed next-generation renewable energy technology, with a focus on cost-effective climate solutions and solid-state energy conversion. His research studied the properties and performance of high-temperature materials for energy generation and storage applications. At Antora, Justin has played nearly every role required to build an energy storage company from the ground up.

fellow_profiles.name: David Bierman
fellow_profiles.bio: David Bierman earned his Ph.D. in mechanical engineering from MIT, where he helped develop a novel solar-driven thermophotovoltaic converter capable of turning heat into electricity by absorbing thermal radiation using a photovoltaic cell. This innovation helped revitalize the field and was featured in a number of media outlets, including Scientific American and IEEE Spectrum. In 2018 he shuttered Marigold Power, to co-found Antora Energy with Andrew Ponec and Justin Briggs.

companies.name: CinderBio
companies.one_liner: CinderBio's technologies enables new ultra-stable enzymes for industrial and laboratory applications.

fellow_profiles.name: Jill Fuss
fellow_profiles.bio: Jill Fuss earned a B.A. in environmental science from Wesleyan University, and a Ph.D. in molecular and cell biology from UC Berkeley. She worked as a postdoctoral fellow and then a research scientist at Berkeley Lab. In 2012, she co-founded CinderBio with fellow Steve Yannone to commercialize extreme enzymes. After leaving CinderBio in 2021, she is now the managing director of the Activate Berkeley Community.

fellow_profiles.name: Steve Yannone
fellow_profiles.bio: Steve Yannone earned a B.S. in biochemistry from UC Riverside and a Ph.D. in molecular biology and biochemistry from UC Irvine followed by a two-year postdoctoral fellowship at Los Alamos National Laboratory and seventeen years leading his group at Berkeley lab as a principal investigator. He and Activate Fellow Jill Fuss co-founded CinderBio in 2012 based on research conducted in his group at Berkeley Lab.

companies.name: Fervo Energy
companies.one_liner: Fervo Energy commercializes technology to develop, own, and operate geothermal assets as the dispatchable foundation to a clean energy future.

fellow_profiles.name: Jack Norbeck
fellow_profiles.bio: Fervo Energy CTO Jack Norbeck is a geothermal reservoir engineer with a background in geomechanics and high-performance computing. Previously, he worked at The Geysers geothermal field, the Raft River geothermal project at Idaho National Laboratory, and the Geothermal Institute at the University of Auckland. Norbeck holds a Ph.D. in energy resources engineering from Stanford University, an M.S. in civil engineering from Colorado School of Mines, and a B.S. in civil engineering from UC Boulder.

fellow_profiles.name: Tim Latimer
fellow_profiles.bio: Fervo Energy CEO Tim Latimer began his career as a drilling engineer with BHP Billiton. Motivated to confront climate change, he left the oil and gas industry in 2015 to pursue an M.B.A. and an M.S. in environment and resources from Stanford University. He holds a degree in mechanical engineering from the University of Tulsa.

companies.name: Noble Thermodynamic Systems
companies.one_liner: Noble Thermodynamics’ closed-loop reciprocating engine recycles a noble gas working fluid and captures all carbon dioxide and other pollutants.

fellow_profiles.name: Miguel Sierra Aznar
fellow_profiles.bio: Miguel Sierra Aznar co-founded Noble Thermodynamic Systems, which is building zero-carbon, dispatchable, and affordable power for the energy sector. Sierra Aznar earned an M.S. from the Technical University of Eindhoven, Netherlands, and a B.S. in engineering with honors from the Hanze University of Applied Sciences in Groningen, Netherlands, as well as a B.S. in power generation and energy conversion systems from the Polytechnic University of Valencia, Spain.

companies.name: Noon Energy
companies.tagline: A new class of ultra-low-cost battery
companies.one_liner: Noon Energy’s flow battery technology enables economical long-duration energy storage of renewable energy to meet continual demand.

nsf_awards.title: SBIR Phase II:  Rechargeable Carbon-Oxygen Battery for Ultra-Low-Cost Renewable Energy Storage
SBIR Phase I:  Rechargeable Carbon-Oxygen Battery for Ultra-Low-Cost Renewable Energy Storage

fellow_profiles.name: Chris Graves
fellow_profiles.bio: After receiving his Ph.D. from Columbia University, Chris Graves continued electrochemistry research at the Technical University of Denmark where he worked as a senior scientist. He founded Noon Energy in 2018 to commercialize a new type of low-cost long-duration battery he invented to enable 100% solar and wind power. Graves has led projects on novel electrochemical energy storage technologies, from new materials to modeling and characterization of cells to design and techno-economic analysis of systems.

companies.name: Sonera
companies.one_liner: Sonera is democratizing portable, high-performance functional brain imaging with dramatically reduced size, weight, power consumption, and cost.

nsf_awards.title: SBIR Phase I:  Evaluating ADFMR magnetometers for portable, non-invasive functional bioimaging

fellow_profiles.name: Dominic Labanowski
fellow_profiles.bio: Dominic Labanowski earned a Ph.D. in electrical engineering and computer sciences from UC Berkeley and a B.S. in electrical and computer engineering from The Ohio State University. He co-founded Sonera with Nishita Deka, whom he met while conducting graduate research, to optimize and develop resonant magneto-acoustic devices with diverse applications including magnetic sensing, antenna miniaturization, and control of quantum systems.

fellow_profiles.name: Nishita Deka
fellow_profiles.bio: Nishita Deka holds a Ph.D. in electrical engineering from UC Berkeley and a B.S. in electrical engineering from the University of Southern California. She co-founded Sonera to commercialize a novel magnetic sensing technology for high-performance, non-invasive neural interfaces with the goal of enabling the next generation of computing, in which humans and technology are more intimately connected.

companies.name: Takachar
companies.tagline: Harvesting value from agricultural & forest waste
companies.one_liner: Takachar can transform massive amounts of waste biomass, much of which is currently burned, into marketable products around the world.

fellow_profiles.name: Kevin Kung
fellow_profiles.bio: Takachar chief technology officer Kevin Kung earned his Ph.D. from MIT, focusing on biofuels and renewable energy, specializing in biomass and energy conversion systems. In 2012, he co-founded Safi Organics, which turns agricultural residues into carbon-negative fertilizer. He then co-founded Takachar to further improve biomass conversion technology. Kung received his A.B. in physics from Princeton University, M.S. in physics from the University of Cambridge, and S.M. in biological engineering from MIT.

companies.name: Dauntless.io
companies.tagline: Real-time, adaptive machine learning for energy applications
companies.one_liner: Dauntless.io uses ultra-fast adaptive machine learning algorithms to cost-effectively control complex energy systems with real-time software and hardware.

fellow_profiles.name: Adam Vaughan
fellow_profiles.bio: Adam is focused on patented Artificial Intelligence (AI) solutions which are deployed to the Fortune 500.

companies.name: Gradient
companies.one_liner: Gradient (formerly TREAU) is rethinking air conditioners with a new high-efficiency design and low-carbon refrigerants.

fellow_profiles.name: Vince Romanin
fellow_profiles.bio: Vince Romanin received his Ph.D. in mechanical engineering from UC Berkeley, with a focus on heat transfer, fluid mechanics, and energy and resources. Before co-founding Gradient and joining the Activate Fellowship, he was a mechanical engineer and research scientist at Otherlab. Prior to Otherlab, Romanin worked with Abengoa Research on advanced heat transfer fluids, properties, and chemistries.

companies.name: Nelumbo
companies.one_liner: Nelumbo brings metamaterials (precise surface morphology or surface structure) with extraordinary properties to a wide range of products.

fellow_profiles.name: David Walther
fellow_profiles.bio: Prior to Nelumbo, Dave Walther spent eight years in research and development, and engineering management roles for Cobalt Technologies, an international renewable chemicals and biofuels company. He spent over 10 years leading research teams in biosensing, power, and energy at the Berkeley Sensor and Actuator Center. Walther holds a B.S. in materials science & engineering and B.S., M.S., and Ph.D. degrees in mechanical engineering from UC Berkeley.

fellow_profiles.name: Lance Brockway
fellow_profiles.bio: Lance Brockway received a Ph.D. in chemical engineering from Texas A&M University in 2014. Following his doctorate work, he was a visiting scholar at the Jet Propulsion Laboratory and a postdoc at a joint venture between UC Berkeley and the National University of Singapore. There, he led R&D and manufacturing teams to reinvent the air conditioner for efficient operation in tropical climates.

companies.name: Photia
companies.one_liner: Photia is developing technology for industrial-scale manufacturing of 3D nanostructured materials for energy applications.

fellow_profiles.name: Joe Geddes
fellow_profiles.bio: Joe Geddes founded Photia to enable rapid manufacturing of advanced materials for energy applications using holographic lithography. Prior to Photia, Geddes was an engineer at the company Rolith, where he made key contributions to the development of Rolling Mask Lithography, a method for making two-dimensional nanostructured coatings. He earned his Ph.D. in engineering science and mechanics from Penn State.

companies.name: Liminal Insights
companies.one_liner: Liminal’s ultrasound and machine learning analytics platform, EchoStat®, delivers advanced inspection and process control throughout multiple battery manufacturing stages.

fellow_profiles.name: Andrew Hsieh
fellow_profiles.bio: Andrew Hsieh is an expert in electrochemistry, materials science, and characterization. During his Ph.D., he developed novel anodes for Li-ion batteries. As a postdoc, Hsieh led two ARPA-E funded projects and co-invented Liminal's ultrasonic battery analysis technology. Hsieh holds a Ph.D. in chemical and materials engineering from Princeton University and a B.S. in chemical and biomolecular engineering from UCLA.

fellow_profiles.name: Barry Van Tassell
fellow_profiles.bio: Barry Van Tassell is the co-founder of Liminal, which uses ultrasound and machine learning in advanced battery diagnostics. Van Tassell holds a Ph.D. from The City College of New York and a B.S. from the University at Buffalo, both in chemical engineering. Van Tassell and Liminal co-founder and CEO Andrew Hsieh co-invented the startup's technology. Van Tassell left Liminal in February, 2023.

companies.name: Mallinda Materials
companies.tagline: Vitrimer matrix composites for the circular economy
companies.one_liner: Mallinda’s infinitely recyclable platform chemistry turns composite materials that were previously unrepairable and unrecyclable into circular products.

fellow_profiles.name: Chris Kaffer
fellow_profiles.bio: Chris Kaffer co-founded Mallinda in 2014 and served as its CEO until 2022. He led the company's funding and commercialization strategies scaling production volumes to the metric ton for delivery to OEMs and tier-1s. He is currently the Program Director for Innovyz USA; founding deep tech companies and providing a full capital solution to build organically sustainable businesses.

fellow_profiles.name: Philip Taynton
fellow_profiles.bio: Philip Taynton holds a Ph.D. in chemistry from the University of Colorado, Boulder, where he began his work on vitrimers, a new class of recyclable polymer technology. He is responsible for the development of Mallinda's technology. Prior to Mallinda, Taynton worked in new product development for Avery Dennison in Pasadena, CA.

companies.name: Sepion Technologies
companies.tagline: Advanced membranes for batteries and water treatment
companies.one_liner: Sepion combines the latest in nanoscience, polymer chemistry, and cell engineering to deliver affordable lithium-metal batteries with disruptive performance.

fellow_profiles.name: Pete Frischmann
fellow_profiles.bio: Peter Frischmann has devoted his career to advanced materials R&D. His background in self-assembly and hybrid organic/inorganic materials gives him a unique skill set for tackling long-standing energy storage problems. He was an Alexander von Humboldt Postdoctoral Fellow at the University of Würzburg and holds a Ph.D. in inorganic chemistry from the University of British Columbia and a B.Sc. in chemistry from Idaho State University.

companies.name: Tandem PV
companies.one_liner: Tandem PV’s mechanically-stacked perovskite and silicon tandem solar panels boost efficiency while reducing manufacturing cost.

fellow_profiles.name: Chris Eberspacher
fellow_profiles.bio: Tandem PV co-founder and managing director Chris Eberspacher pioneered the development of efficient thin-film PV materials. He served as chief technology officer at SoloPower Systems, Hanwha, and at Applied Materials. Eberspacher earned a Ph.D. in applied physics from Stanford University and a BS in physics from the University of Texas.

fellow_profiles.name: Colin Bailie
fellow_profiles.bio: Tandem PV co-founder Colin Bailie holds a B.S. in mechanical engineering from Texas A&M University and a M.S. and Ph.D. in materials science and engineering from Stanford University. He performed seminal research on perovskite tandem solar cells, including the first reports of mechanically-stacked perovskite on Silicon and CIGS tandems and monolithic perovskite on Silicon tandem. Bailie was included in the 2016 Forbes 30 under 30 in Energy list.

companies.name: Tierra Biosciences
companies.one_liner: Tierra Biosciences is accelerating bio-based materials by transforming the way people discover, screen, and develop proteins.

fellow_profiles.name: Zach Sun
fellow_profiles.bio: Zachary Sun founded Tierra Biosciences in 2015. Zach has technical experience in cell-free protein production and synthetic biology and early-stage bio-tools commercialization experience. He holds a Ph.D. in biology from Caltech.

companies.name: CalWave
companies.one_liner: CalWave Power Technologies harnesses the renewable power of ocean waves to produce electricity.

fellow_profiles.name: Marcus Lehmann
fellow_profiles.bio: Marcus Lehmann is CEO and co-founder of CalWave Power Technologies, which is on a mission to unlock the vast and steady carbon-free power from ocean waves worldwide. CalWave was spun out of UC Berkeley's Theoretical & Applied Fluid Dynamics Laboratory, where Lehmann was a visiting scholar. Prior to that, he earned his Ph.D. in mechanical engineering at Hamburg University of Technology.

companies.name: Mosaic Materials
companies.tagline: Advanced materials for a cleaner future
companies.one_liner: Mosaic Materials is dedicated to reducing the cost and environmental impact of fossil fuels through the application of proprietary, highly efficient gas separation technologies.

fellow_profiles.name: Steven Kaye
fellow_profiles.bio: Steven Kaye is an experienced leader in the areas of battery technology and R&D. He was part of Activate's 2015 cohort as co-founder, with fellow Thomas McDonald, of Mosaic Materials. He left Mosaic to lead materials R&D for batteries as part of Apple's special projects group. He is now the chief technology officer at ONE. Kaye earned his Ph.D. in chemistry from UC Berkeley.

fellow_profiles.name: Thomas McDonald
fellow_profiles.bio: Thomas McDonald co-founded Mosaic Materials, which was acquired by Baker Hughes in 2022. He is an expert in the design, synthesis, and use of amine-functionalized solid adsorbents and metal-organic frameworks for gas separations and earned a Ph.D. in chemistry from UC Berkeley and a B.A. in chemistry from Northwestern University.

companies.name: PolySpectra
companies.tagline: Functional materials for advanced additive manufacturing
companies.one_liner: polySpectra makes functional materials for advanced additive manufacturing. Its modular platform enables materials with a broad spectrum of tailored properties.

nsf_awards.title: SBIR Phase II:  Modular Manufacturing - Democratizing Functional Materials Printing
SBIR Phase I:  Modular Manufacturing - Democratizing Functional Materials Printing

fellow_profiles.name: Raymond Weitekamp
fellow_profiles.bio: Raymond Weitekamp founded polySpectra based on his Ph.D. work at Caltech where he made a discovery that led to the COR Alpha resin and an additive manufacturing method called functional lithography. Before earning his Ph.D. in chemistry from Caltech, he earned a B.S. from Princeton University, also in chemistry.

companies.name: Spark Thermionics
companies.tagline: A power plant on a chip
companies.one_liner: Spark Thermionics is building a power plant on a chip: a generator that can efficiently convert any fuel to electricity at 1/10th the size and weight of the state-of-the-art.

fellow_profiles.name: Dan Riley
fellow_profiles.bio: Dan Riley co-founded Spark Thermionics with fellow Jared Schwede to develop and commercialize a high-performance power-plant-on-a-chip technology scalable from watts to megawatts. Riley left Spark in 2017 to join Apple, where is he a senior engineering program manager. Riley earned his Ph.D. from Stanford University.

fellow_profiles.name: Jared Schwede
fellow_profiles.bio: Jared Schwede is co-founder and CEO of Spark Thermionics. Previously, he was a graduate research assistant at Stanford University, where he worked on photon-enhanced thermionic emission, a breakout new method with the potential to overcome theoretical limits on conventional solar energy harvesting. Schwede earned a Ph.D. in physics from Stanford and a B.S. in physics from Yale University.

companies.name: Twelve
companies.tagline: Carbon transformation for a climate positive world
companies.one_liner: Twelve (formerly Opus 12) transforms carbon emissions into critical chemicals, materials and fuels that today are made from fossil fuels.

fellow_profiles.name: Etosha Cave
fellow_profiles.bio: Etosha Cave is co-founder and Chief Scientific Officer of Twelve (formerly Opus 12) and an expert in the electrochemical conversion of carbon dioxide into fuels and high-value chemicals. She holds a Ph.D. in mechanical engineering from Stanford and has been an Accel Innovation Scholar, an Echoing Green Fellow, and a Smithsonian Institution Innovators to Watch. In 2018, Vanity Fair named Cave one of 26 Women of Color Diversifying Entrepreneurship in Silicon Valley, Media, and Beyond.

fellow_profiles.name: Kendra Kuhl
fellow_profiles.bio: Kendra Kuhl is co-founder and chief technology officer at Twelve (formerly Opus 12). She and Cohort 2015 fellow Etosha Cave launched Twelve after earning Ph.D.s in the Jaramillo Lab at Stanford University. Kuhl was named to MIT Technology Review's Innovators Under 35 list and The New York Times named her a visionary leader addressing climate change. Kuhl earned a B.S. from the University of Montana, both in chemistry.

companies.name: Visolis
companies.one_liner: Visolis uses synthetic biology and traditional chemical catalysis to produce bio-based chemicals, from drop-in commodity chemicals to innovative specialty offerings.

nsf_awards.title: SBIR Phase II:  High performance monomers from biomass
STTR Phase I:  High performance polymers from biomass

fellow_profiles.name: Deepak Dugar
fellow_profiles.bio: Deepak Dugar is the founder of Visolis and brings deep expertise in bio-based products domain. Previously, he worked as a management consultant at PwC and consulted for DSM, NREL, Flagship Ventures and others. Dugar received the Legatum Fellowship and the Martin family society for sustainability fellowship. Dugar bachelor's and master's degrees in biochemical engineering and biotechnology from IIT Delhi and MBA, M.S. and Ph.D. in chemical engineering practice from MIT.
